# Ma'at

> A documentation-as-code framework that keeps a repository's docs, its cross-agent instruction files, and its source code in lockstep — maintainable by AI agents, human developers, and CI alike.

This file indexes the project's documentation for AI agents and tools. Each link points to a Markdown document; agents should read the ones relevant to the task before making changes.

## Architecture — how the system is built
- [Check engine module](docs/architecture/modules/check.md): The validation rules behind `maat check` — the CI gate.
- [Model & generators module](docs/architecture/modules/model-generate.md): Scanning the docs tree into a model and rendering derived artifacts from it.
- [YAML & front-matter module](docs/architecture/modules/yaml-frontmatter.md): The dependency-free parsing layer — a YAML subset and Markdown front-matter I/O.
- [Architecture Overview](docs/architecture/overview.md): How Ma'at is built — the CLI pipeline, the docs model, and the generate/validate split.

## Decisions — why it is built that way (ADRs)
- [0001 Record architecture decisions](docs/decisions/0001-record-architecture-decisions.md): We will use Architecture Decision Records to document significant choices.
- [0002 Zero runtime dependencies](docs/decisions/0002-zero-dependencies.md): Superseded by ADR 0005. The zero-runtime-dependency goal is preserved by the Go static binary; the stdlib-only-Python mechanism is retired.
- [0003 AGENTS.md as source of truth with generated adapters](docs/decisions/0003-agents-md-source-of-truth.md): One canonical instruction file (AGENTS.md); every other agent's file is generated from it and verified in CI.
- [0004 Detect staleness via related_code timestamps](docs/decisions/0004-related-code-staleness.md): Docs declare the source files they describe; check compares mtimes to flag docs that may lag behind code.
- [0005 Rewrite the CLI in Go, distribute a single static binary](docs/decisions/0005-go-rewrite.md): The CLI is rewritten in Go and shipped as one static binary; the Python reference is retired. Supersedes 0002.
- [0006 Distribution and version pinning](docs/decisions/0006-distribution-and-versioning.md): Ma'at is adopted through a CI gate as the authority, a universal curl|sh installer plus published Action/reusable workflow, and an in-config maat_version constraint the released binary self-enforces.
- [0007 Agent skills as managed artifacts](docs/decisions/0007-agent-skills-as-managed-artifacts.md): Ma'at ships agent skills as version-stamped copies under .maat/skills/, generated from templates embedded in the binary, kept fresh by sync/check, and discovered through AGENTS.md with optional vendor fan-out.
- [0008 Brownfield adoption and BYO-agent retrospection](docs/decisions/0008-brownfield-adoption-byo-agent.md): Adopting Ma'at in an existing repository is non-destructive by default; deriving documentation from an established codebase is delegated to the user's own AI agent via a shipped retrospect skill, with the CLI providing only deterministic facts.
- [Architecture Decision Records](docs/decisions/README.md): Index of ADRs — the append-only log of why the system is the way it is.

## Guides — how to work on it
- [Adopting Ma'at in a repository](docs/guides/deployment.md): How to add Ma'at to an existing project and wire it into CI.
- [Development guide](docs/guides/development.md): How to set up, run, and contribute to Ma'at locally.
- [Testing guide](docs/guides/testing.md): How to run and write Ma'at's tests, and what CI enforces.
- [Troubleshooting](docs/guides/troubleshooting.md): Common maat check failures and how to resolve them.

## Reference — factual surface (config, env, API)
- [CLI reference](docs/reference/cli.md): Every maat command, flag, and exit code.
- [Configuration reference](docs/reference/configuration.md): Every .maat.yml key, its default, and its effect.
- [Environment variables](docs/reference/environment.md): Environment variables Ma'at reads.
- [Front-matter reference](docs/reference/frontmatter.md): The YAML front-matter schema every doc in docs/ carries.

## Meta — conventions and glossary
- [Documentation conventions](docs/meta/conventions.md): The rules this documentation set follows so it stays consistent.
- [Glossary](docs/meta/glossary.md): Definitions of Ma'at's domain terms.
- [Maintaining the documentation](docs/meta/maintenance.md): How the docs stay in sync with the code — the lifecycle and tooling.

## Entry points
- [Ma'at Documentation](docs/index.md): Entry point and map for the Ma'at documentation set.
